extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC won from reduced GC group sprint with bonus seconds, SEPP KUSS took red jersey after controlling finale and dropping MARTINEZ on final climb.
tactical_patterns: SEPP KUSS neutralized attacks while teammates VINGEGAARD and ROGLIC collected stage bonus seconds, perfect combined leadership tactic.
Early breakaway of 30 riders internally fractured on crosswinds before first climb, revealing size creates vulnerability not strength.
EVENEPOEL rode front excessively into finale, sacrificed sprint position despite controlling race, tactical execution error despite fitness.
MARTINEZ lost red jersey and over minute on final wall climb, exposed gap between one-week and three-week conditioning.

rider_signals.SEPP KUSS: positive: Controlled GC group personally while teammates took stage honors, showing genuine Grand Tour leadership capacity not just domestique form.
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Won stage sprint after multiple mountain efforts, indicates layered form beyond typical one-dimensional climber preparing.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Dominated race tactically but poor sprint execution from front suggests inexperience translating control into results.
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Lenny: negative: Lost over minute and jersey on short steep wall, signals unsustainable early race positioning.
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Recovered from early climb difficulty to finish with GC group, deep endurance compensating for explosive weakness.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Advanced to fifth overall after being distanced in breakaway earlier, pacing strategy superior to early aggression.
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: Finished second overall at stage end, quietly consistent while others attacked and faded.
